description_long: This object consists of two irregularly shaped rocks resting on a flat, textured stone base. The rocks are medium-sized, with rough, jagged surfaces and a predominantly gray coloration, featuring some lighter and darker patches. The base appears to be a slab of stone, slightly uneven and blending visually with the rocks, suggesting a natural or minimally altered setting. The rocks have a weathered appearance, with visible pitting and surface granularity, indicating exposure to the elements. There are no visible markings, graffiti, or artificial modifications on the rocks or the base. The overall composition suggests these rocks could serve as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: Two rough, gray rocks rest on a flat stone base, with weathered, jagged surfaces and natural coloration.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the two rocks from a slightly elevated angle, highlighting the larger rock's rough top and the smaller rock's lighter color.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the rocks from the side, emphasizing the elongated shape of the base and the relative size difference between the two rocks.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the rocks from the opposite side, showing the flatter surfaces and the way the rocks nestle into the base.
description_view_3: View 3 offers a rear perspective, focusing on the back and side textures of the rocks and the uneven edge of the stone base.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
